title: Fix RawFileSlice idx and tags in lexer_test
body:
While running the lexer test in rust mode, these `RawFileSlice` tags came across with type errors. This fixes the order of the `block_idx` and `tag`.

No other side effects.

step_plan.summary: Correct the order of the last two positional arguments when constructing RawFileSlice instances in test/core/parser/lexer_test.py so block_idx (int) is passed before tag (Optional[str]). This fixes type errors in the lexer tests without changing production code.

steps.operation: Fix RawFileSlice argument order in test fixtures used by the parameterized test
steps.reason: The RawFileSlice NamedTuple is defined as RawFileSlice(raw, slice_type, source_idx, block_idx: int = 0, tag: Optional[str] = None). In the test fixtures the last two positional arguments are reversed (e.g. (..., source_idx, None, 0)), which assigns None to block_idx and 0 to tag and causes type errors. Swap the 4th and 5th arguments for all RawFileSlice instantiations in the TemplatedFile cases so they follow (source_idx, 0, None), or replace with explicit keyword args (block_idx=0, tag=None) to ensure correct typing and clarity.
steps.side_effects: This change only touches the test file and should resolve type errors when running the lexer tests. No production code or API changes. Tests that expect the previous (incorrectly-typed) fixtures may need updates if they asserted on the raw representation of RawFileSlice (unlikely). Running the test suite may reveal other tests that instantiate RawFileSlice with misordered positional args; those would need similar fixes.
